title: Peltanthera costaricensis
intro: Peltanthera costaricensis is a species of orchid native to Costa Rica, characterized by its unique, small-sized flowers and delicate foliage. Traditionally, Peltanthera costaricensis has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Peltanthera costaricensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Peltanthera costaricensis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Peltanthera costaricensis is classified under the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification suggests that Peltanthera costaricensis is closely related to other orchid species. Within the Orchidaceae family, Peltanthera costaricensis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Vanilloideae. The subfamily Vanilloideae is a group of orchids that are primarily found in Central and South America. Peltanthera costaricensis is specifically classified under the genus Peltanthera, which is a small genus of orchids.
